_meta.description: Active-ingredient reference for OnLabel. Adult OTC max daily doses. Values grounded in FDA/OTC labeling; entries with verify=true need DailyMed/openFDA confirmation.
_meta.source: FDA OTC labeling; GetReliefResponsibly (FDA campaign); Drugs.com combination dosing

acetaminophen.conservativeSource: Extra Strength Tylenol label — McNeil lowered the stated daily max to 3,000 mg/24h (2011); the FDA monograph ceiling remains 4,000 mg/24h
acetaminophen.risk: Liver damage — the leading cause of acute liver failure in the US. Often hidden in multi-symptom cold/flu and PM products.

dosing.intervalText: every 4 to 6 hours (650–1,000 mg every 6 hours, or 325–650 mg every 4 hours)
dosing.maxDurationText: do not use for more than 10 days for pain or 3 days for fever unless directed by a doctor
dosing.source: FDA OTC Monograph M013 §M013.20(b)(2) Directions; 10-day limit §M013.20(b) labeling

ibuprofen.risk: GI bleeding, kidney and cardiovascular risk. Do not combine with other NSAIDs.
ibuprofen.source: FDA OTC label (1,200 mg/24h; Rx allows more)

naproxen.risk: GI bleeding, kidney and cardiovascular risk. Do not combine with other NSAIDs.

aspirin.risk: GI bleeding; Reye's syndrome in children/teens with viral illness. Is an NSAID.
aspirin.source: FDA OTC Monograph M013 (Internal Analgesic) §M013.10(b) Dosage: adults not to exceed 4,000 mg in 24 hours.

dosing.intervalText: every 4 to 6 hours (325–650 mg every 4 hours, or 650–1,000 mg every 6 hours)
dosing.maxDurationText: do not use for more than 10 days for pain or 3 days for fever unless directed by a doctor
dosing.source: FDA OTC Monograph M013 §M013.20(b)(2) Directions; 10-day limit §M013.20(b)

dextromethorphan.risk: Excess causes neurologic effects. Common in cough + cold combos.
dextromethorphan.source: FDA OTC Cough/Cold/Allergy monograph (M012); Drugs.com professional monograph — 120 mg/24h

dosing.intervalText: 10–20 mg every 4 hours, or 30 mg every 6 to 8 hours
dosing.maxDurationText: if cough lasts more than 7 days (adults), see a doctor
dosing.source: FDA OTC Monograph M012 §M012.14 Directions; cough duration §M012 antitussive labeling

guaifenesin.source: FDA OTC Cough/Cold/Allergy monograph (M012); Drugs.com professional monograph — 2,400 mg/24h

dosing.maxDurationText: if cough lasts more than 7 days (adults), see a doctor

pseudoephedrine.risk: Raises blood pressure and heart rate. Sold behind the counter.
pseudoephedrine.source: FDA OTC Monograph M012 (Cough/Cold/Allergy) §M012.20(a)(2)-(3) Directions: adults 60 mg every 4-6h, not to exceed 240 mg in 24 hours.

dosing.maxDurationText: do not use for more than 7 days
dosing.source: FDA OTC Monograph M012 §M012.20(a)(2) Directions; 7-day limit §M012 nasal decongestant labeling

phenylephrine.efficacyNote: An FDA advisory committee unanimously (16-0) concluded in Sept 2023 that oral phenylephrine is NOT effective as a nasal decongestant; FDA proposed removing it from the OTC monograph (Nov 8, 2024).
phenylephrine.source: FDA OTC Monograph M012 (Cough/Cold/Allergy) §M012.20(a)(1) Directions: adults 10 mg every 4h, not to exceed 60 mg in 24 hours. Efficacy per FDA 2023-2024 review.

dosing.maxDurationText: do not use for more than 7 days
dosing.source: FDA OTC Monograph M012 §M012.20(a)(1) Directions; 7-day limit §M012 nasal decongestant labeling

diphenhydramine.risk: Sedation and anticholinergic effects; additive with other sedating antihistamines and alcohol.

chlorpheniramine.risk: Sedation; additive with other sedating antihistamines.
chlorpheniramine.source: OTC antihistamine labeling (4 mg q4-6h, max 24 mg/24h)

doxylamine.risk: Sedation; found in nighttime cold products and sleep aids.
doxylamine.source: FDA OTC Monograph M012 (Cough/Cold/Allergy) §M012.12(h) Directions: adults 7.5-12.5 mg every 4-6h, not to exceed 75 mg in 24 hours.

fexofenadine.source: OTC label — 180 mg once daily (or 60 mg twice daily)

caffeine.risk: Added to some analgesics; jitteriness at high totals.
caffeine.source: FDA OTC Monograph M011 (Stimulant) §M011.50(d) Directions: adults 100-200 mg per dose, not more often than every 3-4h. The monograph states no 24-hour ceiling, so maxDailyMg is intentionally null (duplication is still flagged).
